response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack and web-platform engineer with 15+ years of experience, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. C++ appears only in a 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2015 and a student thesis project — it is absent from his current skills list entirely. His primary stack is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. There is no evidence of industrial automation domain knowledge, IEC 62443 secure development lifecycle practices, MQTT, or industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850), all of which are core to this position.
strengths: Broad software engineering background spanning 15+ years with demonstrated ability to work across multiple languages and paradigms
Hands-on event-driven and distributed systems experience (Sociomantic high-throughput microservices, GISCollective event-driven geo-platform)
Agile engineering practices well established: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and mentoring via workshops and dojos
concerns: C++ competency is minimal and over a decade stale — not listed in current skills and represents only ~6 months of professional experience
No industrial automation, IoT, or embedded systems background; no exposure to IEC 62443, IEC 61850, Modbus, OPC UA, MQTT, or DNP 3.0
Current and recent work is entirely web/cloud-native (TypeScript, Node.js, AWS CDK, React), with no secure development lifecycle (SDL) experience relevant to industrial cybersecurity
